title: Proximity-induced orbital antiferromagnetism in Ising superconductors
authors: G. A. Bobkov, V. A. Bobkov, T. Karabassov, I. V. Bobkova, A. A. Golubov
abstract: We predict a fundamentally new superconducting state in superconductor/antiferromagnet heterostructures with Ising spin--orbit coupling: proximity-induced orbital antiferromagnetism. In this state, the order parameter acquires a periodic phase modulation locked to the magnetic lattice, generating atomic-scale loop currents with opposite orbital moments on neighboring unit cells. Its emergence requires at least three nonequivalent magnetic sublattices per unit cell and finite spin--orbit coupling. Using NbSe$_2$/MnPS$_3$ as a concrete example, we combine first-principles and Bogoliubov--de Gennes calculations to demonstrate that the proximity-induced exchange field leads to robust phase modulation. Unlike FFLO and helical states, the phase gradient is atomic-scale, the state is current-carrying, and it remains uniquely stable over the full parameter range. The state manifests as characteristic finite-energy dips in the local density of states, accessible by STM.
summary: The paper predicts a novel superconducting state called orbital antiferromagnetism in superconductor/antiferromagnet interfaces. This state is characterized by an atomic-scale periodic modulation of the superconducting order parameter, generating persistent loop currents. The theoretical framework combines DFT and BdG calculations to predict observable signatures like specific dips in the local density of states.
main_problem: The prediction of a fundamentally new superconducting state—orbital antiferromagnetism—in superconductor/antiferromagnet heterostructures.
main_result: The predicted state is unique, exhibiting atomic-scale loop currents and remaining stable over the full parameter range, unlike FFLO or helical states.
method: Combining first-principles electronic structure (DFT) with Bogoliubov–de Gennes (BdG) calculations to analyze superconducting order parameter modulation.
model_or_system: The system is an S/AF heterostructure, exemplified by NbSe2/MnPS3. The physics is governed by a tight-binding Hamiltonian incorporating proximity-induced exchange fields and Ising spin–orbit coupling.
key_observables: Characteristic finite-energy dips in the local density of states (LDOS); periodic phase modulation of the superconducting order parameter ($\Delta_i$).
important_parameters: The ratio of induced exchange field to superconducting gap ($|\mathbf{h}|/\Delta$); interface transparency ($D$).
main_assumptions: The analysis often assumes $|\mathbf{h}_{A,B,C}| \ll \Delta$ for analytical treatments, though numerical solutions are used when this is violated.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the heterostructure geometry and current loops; electronic band structures show folded Brillouin zones; LDOS plots reveal finite-energy dips corresponding to OP inhomogeneity.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from defining the physical problem in S/AF heterostructures, developing the Hamiltonian and theoretical framework using Green's functions, calculating the superconducting order parameter modulation, and finally analyzing related phenomena like triplet correlations based on magnetic field type (ferri vs. antiferro).
why_interesting: This work deals with exotic pairing mechanisms and orbital magnetism induced by proximity effects in layered materials, which are central topics in modern condensed matter theory relevant to strongly correlated systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Top view of the NbSe2 and MnPS3 monolayers and side views of the NbSe2/MnPS3 heterostructure in the (x, z) and (y, z) planes. Black arrows in the side views and circles/crosses in the top view indicate Mn magnetic moments aligned in +z/ −z directions respectively. (b) Schematic of atomic-scale current loops with opposite circulation in the NbSe2 monolayer, induced by the phase difference of the superconducting order parameter on the Nb sites A, B, and C. Red arrows indicate the current flow between neighboring sites. (c) Brillouin zone and the spin-split Fermi surfaces of the isolated NbSe2 monolayer. The dashed curve marks the boundary of the folded Brillouin zone of the...

FIG. 2. (a) Electronic band structure of the NbSe2/MnPS3 heterostructure in the folded Brillouin zone [see Fig. fig:1(d)]. Blue branches stem from NbSe2; red branches are of MnPS3 origin, which retains its insulating character in the het- erostructure upon inclusion of interlayer charge transfer. (b)– (c) Zoom-in on the vicinity of the Kr point (b) for a free- standing NbSe2 monolayer and (c) for the NbSe2/MnPS3 het- erostructure. Solid and dashed lines correspond to dpin-up and spin-down branches.

FIG. 3. Superconducting OP in the NbSe2 monolayer with the effective exchange field hA,B,C = D{+8, −10.5, 0} meV as a function of temperature. (a) OP amplitude (identical on all three magnetic sublattices); (b) OP phase on the three magnetic sublattices: φA (dashed), φB (dotted), φC (solid).

FIG. 4. (a) Spin-resolved LDOS (red: up, blue: down) of the superconducting monolayer in an effective exchange field. Inset: zoom of the superconducting gap at the Fermi-level. Finite-energy dips due to the periodic inhomogeneity of the superconducting OP are marked by arrows. (b) Zoom of one such dip region. NF is the normal-state LDOS at E = 0.

Fig. 3(b) demonstrates a phase modulation φA,B,C with magnetic periodicity for any nonzero exchange field. The phase is linear, to good accuracy, in hA,B,C (even beyond the h ≪∆analytical limit) and temperature- independent, contrasting sharply with the FFLO state.
